title: <name> and the Peacock and the Rainbow Path

pages.text: One golden morning in the town of Udaipur, <name> was watering Amma’s marigold plants when a flash of blue and green shimmered before her eyes. A magnificent peacock stood in her backyard, its feathers sparkling like tiny jewels.

“Namaste, <name>,” said the peacock with a gentle bow. “I am Pari, the Peacock of Rainbows. The colors of the sky have disappeared! Will you help me find them?” <name> clapped her hands. “Of course! But where do we start?” Pari spread his feathers wide, and suddenly, a bright rainbow path appeared under their feet. “Follow me!” he said.

They stepped into an enchanted forest where golden sunlight danced through singing trees. The leaves hummed soft tunes — la-la-la — and glowing lotus ponds shimmered nearby. Friendly monkeys swung from vines, chattering, “Looking for colors, are you? Solve our riddle, and we’ll show you where they hide!”

One monkey grinned and asked, “What shines without fire, and dances after rain?” <name> smiled. “A rainbow!” The monkeys cheered and pointed toward a silver waterfall. At its base lay a shimmering crown, dull and colorless. “The Crown of Seasons!” gasped Pari. “It controls the colors of the world — but it’s lost its light!”

<name> knelt beside it. “Maybe it needs kindness.” She plucked one marigold from her basket — Amma’s favorite flower — and placed it on the crown. At once, the petals glowed! The waterfall glittered, and colors burst across the sky — red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, violet — twirling together into a dazzling rainbow.

Pari laughed joyfully. “You did it, <name>! You brought the colors back with your heart.” As the forest sparkled, Pari gently placed one glowing feather in <name>’s hand. “A gift,” he said. “Whenever you miss the rainbow, this will remind you that kindness brings color to the world.”

When <name> returned home, the marigolds seemed brighter, and a soft rainbow arched above her garden. She smiled, holding the feather close. “Thank you, Pari,” she whispered. And far away, in the forest, a peacock’s happy call echoed through the clouds.
